context: Tests whether the agent captures detailed typographic characteristics from the fetched pages — specifically the typeface category (serif/sans/display), weight usage, and tracking/letter-spacing — in visual.md, beyond just a pairing recommendation. Also checks that photo direction and iconography sections are present.

checklist.name: Typeface category identified
checklist.description: visual.md states whether the brand's primary typeface is serif, sans-serif, or display (or a combination), not just a font name or 'modern'

checklist.name: Type weight described
checklist.description: visual.md describes the font weight(s) used by the brand (e.g., bold, regular, light, heavy, semi-bold) — not just the font family

checklist.name: Tracking or letter-spacing noted
checklist.description: visual.md notes how the brand uses letter-spacing or tracking — e.g., 'tight tracking on headlines', 'wide-tracked all-caps labels', 'normal tracking throughout'

checklist.name: Typography pairing recommendation
checklist.description: visual.md includes a pairing recommendation — how the heading and body typefaces relate to each other (e.g., 'bold display paired with a clean sans body')

checklist.name: Photo direction
checklist.description: visual.md contains a photo direction section describing the style of photography appropriate for the brand (lighting, mood, subject, setting)

checklist.name: Iconography rules
checklist.description: visual.md contains a section on iconography or illustration style used by the brand

checklist.name: palette.json — six color roles
checklist.description: palette.json contains exactly the six required top-level keys: primary, secondary, page_bg, card_bg, text, muted

checklist.name: palette.json — hex values present
checklist.description: Each of the six color entries in palette.json has a 'hex' field containing a color value (not a placeholder like #XXXXXX)
